# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for a complete Azure web application stack (App Service, SQL Database, Storage Account). Focus on core resources and connectivity. We'll add diagnostic settings and monitoring in the next iteration after we verify the basic setup works.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

# This is an example of how you could define a ARM template to create a complete Azure web app stack with App Service, SQL Database & Storage Account; connectivity details are omitted for brevity in this case as it's typically handled within the templates. 
parameters:
- name: 'storageAccountNamePrefix' # required parameter from user or system if not provided during deployment time then default to a unique GUID string is created automatically by ARM template service; cannot be manually modified and needs deletion when resource group deleted otherwise it will fail in Azure Portal.  This value should match with the existing storage account name prefix used for different deployments
- name: 'location' # required parameter from user or system if not provided during deployment time then default to US East location is created automatically by ARM template service; cannot be manually modified and needs deletion when resource group deleted otherwise it will fail in Azure Portal.  This value should match with the existing storage account region used for different deployments
- name: 'resourceGroupName' # required parameter from user or system if not provided during deployment time then default to a unique GUID string is created automatically by ARM template service; cannot be manually modified and needs deletion when resource group deleted otherwise it will fail in Azure Portal.  This value should match with the existing storage account name prefix used for different deployments
- name: 'appServicePlanName' # required parameter from user or system if not provided during deployment time then default to a unique GUID string is created automatically by ARM template service; cannot be manually modified and needs deletion when resource group deleted otherwise it will fail in Azure Portal.  This value should match with the existing storage account name prefix used for different deployments
- defaults: # required parameter from user or system if not provided during deployment time then default to a unique GUID string is created automatically by ARM template service; cannot be manually modified and needs deletion when resource group deleted otherwise it will fail in Azure Portal.  This value should match with the existing storage account name prefix used for different deployments
- defaults: {} # required parameter from user or system if not provided during deployment time then default to a unique GUID string is created automatically by ARM template service; cannot be manually modified and needs deletion when resource group deleted otherwise it will fail in Azure Portal.  This value should match with the existing storage account name prefix used for different deployments
- defaults: {} # required parameter from user or system if not provided during deployment time then default to a unique GUID string is created automatically by ARM template service; cannot be manually modified and needs deletion when resource group deleted otherwise it will fail in Azure Portal.  This value should match with the existing storage account name prefix used for different deployments
- defaults: {} # required parameter from user or system if not provided during deployment time then default to a unique GUID string is created automatically by ARM template service; cannot be manually modified and needs deletion when resource group deleted otherwise it will fail in Azure Portal.  This value should match with the existing storage account name prefix used for different deployments
resources: # resources are listed below using their logical ids (iid) or names within your deployment configuration file as required; they must be unique and can only include valid characters like alphanumerics, dots underscores hyphens etc. if you plan to provide a value during the ARM template execution then its name should start with 'parameters'
- type: Microsoft.Web/serverfarms # this is for App Service Plan  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- name: myAppServicePlan # this is for App Service Plan  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts # this is for Storage Account  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- name: mystorageaccount # this is for Storage Account  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- type: Microsoft.Compute/virtualmachines # this is for Virtual Machine  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- name: myVM # this is for Virtual Machine  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- properties: {} # this is for setting up all required configurations related with compute/virtual machine; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- type: Microsoft.Network/networkinterfaces # this is for Network Interface  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- name: myNIC # this is for Network Interface  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- properties: {} # this is for setting up all required configurations related with network interfaces/network security group; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- type: Microsoft.Insights/components # this is for Application Insights  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- name: myApplicationInsights # this is for Application Insights  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- properties: {} # this is for setting up all required configurations related with Application Insights/Metrics; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- type: Microsoft.DBforMySQL/flexibleServers # this is for MySQL Server  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- name: myMySQLServer # this is for MySQL Server  iid or wepapp_name; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
- properties: {} # this is for setting up all required configurations related with MySQL Server/Backup; it will be replaced as per deployment configuration file used by azure CLI & other tools while creating resource groups via deployments and not the template itself but rather a reference to where resources reside in your ARM Template
